contractions.rule511Body: Contractions ~5 min apart, ~1 min long, sustained. Most providers recommend heading to your birth location now. Call your midwife or OB.

hydration.tip: Small, frequent sips are better than large amounts. Ice chips and popsicles count too!
hydration.suggestionRecommend: Doulas recommend sipping every {{minutes}} min at this stage

relief.sortedFor: Sorted for {{phase}} — most relevant methods first
relief.mediaPlaceholder: Media URL: image, YouTube, Spotify, or any link (optional)

tracking.title: Building Your Pattern
tracking.meaning: Log a few more contractions to see your pattern emerge.
tracking.tip: Rest comfortably, move gently, and stay hydrated.

early.title: Early Labor
early.meaning: Cervix beginning to dilate (0-6 cm). Contractions are becoming more regular — this phase can last many hours.
early.tip: Rest when you can, take gentle walks, eat light snacks. You can typically manage comfortably at home.

active.title: Active Labor
active.meaning: Likely 6-8 cm dilated. Contractions stronger and more consistent — many people head to their birth location now.
active.tip: Focus on breathing, change positions every 20-30 min. Contact your midwife or OB if you haven't.

transition.title: Transition
transition.meaning: The final, most intense phase — and the shortest. Likely 8-10 cm. Your baby is almost here!
transition.tip: One contraction at a time. Breathe, vocalize, stay grounded. You are so close!

expecting.setDueDatePrompt: Set your due date to start the countdown
expecting.noTasks: No tasks yet — add one above

settings.title: Settings
settings.notifDeniedHint: Notifications blocked — enable them in your browser settings.
settings.cloudSyncPrivacyNote: No account needed. Your data is stored anonymously.

notifications.waterBody: Your water reminder interval has ended.

labourStarted.title: Labour has started!
labourStarted.body: Your partner has switched to Labour mode. Head to the app.

contacts.title: Labour Contacts
contacts.noContacts: No contacts yet. Add your midwife, doula, or partner.
